info.description:
The seo-studio API powers the local proof-of-concept workflow for image optimization,
website crawling, AI metadata generation, and exports.

The POC uses local file storage, a separate local Ollama runtime, and Supabase
Auth for dashboard access. Long-running worker queues and persistent job
databases are intentionally deferred until beta readiness.
info.summary: Local API for the seo-studio optimization POC.
info.title: seo-studio API

get.description: Returns the Supabase user represented by the provided bearer token.

post.description: Creates an image job from one or more uploaded files. Accepts direct `.jpg`, `.jpeg`, `.png`, and `.webp` images plus `.zip` archives that contain supported images. Direct image files and ZIP entries are limited to 5MB each. ZIP archives are safely inspected to prevent path traversal.

400.description: The upload is empty, unsupported, too large, unsafe, or not a valid image.

get.description: Returns status for an image job created by the upload endpoint, including the number of validated uploaded image files.

Returns brand context documents attached to an image job and the extracted plain text that will be included in AI image metadata prompts. Empty documents and text are returned when no brand context has been uploaded.

post.description: Attaches one or more brand context documents to an image job. Supports `.txt`, `.docx`, and `.pdf` files. The backend stores the original documents under the job folder, extracts plain text, truncates the combined context to the configured prompt limit, and persists it in `storage/uploads/{job_id}/job.json` for later AI metadata generation.

400.description: The upload is empty, unsupported, too large, or has no extractable text.

get.description: Returns broken links for a website job. Phase 9 will include status codes, source pages, timeout handling, and internal/external classification.

Creates and downloads a ZIP archive containing every processed image for an image job. The job must already be processed through the image optimizer. Images are stored inside the archive under the `images/` folder.

404.description: Job or processed images were not found.

get.description: Placeholder export endpoint for CSV, JSON, and XLSX downloads. Phase 7 will return generated files with the correct media type.
Returns validated direct image uploads and extracted ZIP image entries for an image job.
Returns generated AI metadata for an image job. Empty results are returned when metadata has not been generated yet.

post.description: Generates filename suggestions, alt text, captions, and confidence scores for every image in a job. Images are processed sequentially through the configured AI provider. Failed rows are returned with failed status while successful rows are persisted for review.
post.summary: Generate AI metadata for all image files

get.description: Exports one CSV row per uploaded image in a job. Optional repeated `fields` query values control which SEO and file columns are included. The export joins uploaded image records, generated AI metadata when available, and processed image filenames when the job has been optimized.

parameters.description: CSV fields to include. Repeat this query value for multiple columns.
parameters.name: fields

schema.description: CSV fields to include. Repeat this query value for multiple columns.
schema.title: Fields

400.description: One or more requested CSV fields are not supported.

get.description: Creates a ZIP archive for selected uploaded image rows. The archive includes renamed image files under `images/` and a `report.csv` file with metadata for only the selected rows. Repeated `image_ids` query values choose which images are included.

parameters.description: Uploaded image file ids to include. Repeat this query value for multiple rows.
parameters.name: image_ids

schema.description: Uploaded image file ids to include. Repeat this query value for multiple rows.
schema.title: Image Ids

404.description: Job, selected image, or source image was not found.

get.summary: Export selected AI metadata images ZIP

get.description: Downloads the processed image for an uploaded image id when processed output exists. If the job has not been processed, downloads the original uploaded image instead. The attachment filename uses the provided filename query value, generated AI filename, or stored filename while preserving the actual downloaded file extension.

parameters.description: Optional extension-free filename to use for the attachment.
parameters.name: filename

schema.description: Optional extension-free filename to use for the attachment.
schema.title: Filename

404.description: Job, image, processed file, or original upload not found.

post.description: Regenerates filename suggestion, alt text, caption, and confidence score for one uploaded image in an image job.

404.description: Job or image file not found.

post.summary: Regenerate AI metadata for one image

get.description: Returns link check results for a website job. Phase 9 will populate this with internal and external link status data.
Returns pages discovered for a website job. Phase 8 will populate this after same-domain crawling and content extraction.

post.description: Processes every validated image in an image job using the provided compression, conversion, and filename cleanup settings. Defaults match the RFC: lossy mode, quality 80, no resize, metadata stripping enabled, and original output format. It can convert output format to WebP, JPG, PNG, or keep the original extension. Optional filename overrides are sanitized, lowercased, de-duplicated, and saved with the selected output extension. Processed files are written to `storage/processed/{job_id}/images`. Transparent images converted to JPG are flattened onto a white background.

schema.description: Processing settings. Quality is most relevant for JPEG and WebP; PNG uses lossless optimization behavior. filename_overrides may be used to manually set cleaned output filenames by uploaded file id.

400.description: The job has no images or settings are invalid.

404.description: Job or uploaded source file not found.

get.description: Downloads one processed image from `storage/processed/{job_id}/images`. The filename must be a direct processed filename returned by the compression result.

post.description: Requests AI vision crop suggestions for an exact resize instruction. This endpoint is intentionally separate from resize instruction parsing because model calls can be slow or fail. The response returns updated processing settings with optional crop boxes, plus notes or warnings that the UI can show before crop review.

schema.description: Subject-aware crop request and current resize settings.

400.description: Exact target width and height are required.

404.description: Job or uploaded source file not found.

502.description: Configured AI provider request failed or returned invalid crop JSON.

post.description: Converts a natural-language resize request into editable image processing settings. The POC parser recognizes common dimensions such as `600 x 400`, output formats like WebP/JPG/PNG, lossless mode, quality values, and fit-inside language. This endpoint does not call the AI model; subject-aware AI crop suggestions are requested separately through the AI crop endpoint so parsing stays responsive.

schema.description: Natural-language resize request to interpret for the image job.

post.description: Inspects uploaded images against exact resize settings and returns per-image crop review suggestions. Images whose aspect ratio does not match the target ratio are marked for review before final processing. Suggestions include normalized crop focus coordinates and optional AI crop boxes that can be reviewed in the UI before final processing.

schema.description: Resize settings used to determine crop review requirements.

400.description: Exact target width and height are required.

404.description: Job or uploaded source file not found.

get.description: Returns local runtime settings used by the frontend, including the Ollama endpoint, default model, CORS origin, and backend storage root.
Returns a small response confirming the FastAPI service is running.
